notes: All 10 assertions pass for both with_skill and without_skill (100% vs 100%) — current assertions do NOT discriminate skill value

CRITICAL UNCAUGHT BUG: fr-without_skill translated placeholder text inside a plain (non-R) code block: <DATA>→<DONNÉES>, <MAPPINGS>→<CORRESPONDANCES>, <GEOM_FUNCTION>()→<FONCTION_GEOM>(). The with_skill run correctly preserved this. No current assertion covers plain code blocks.
Recommended new assertion: 'plain-code-blocks-preserved' — verifies that plain ``` blocks (without {r ...}) are preserved verbatim
With-skill runs take ~12s longer on average (48.4s vs 36.5s) — the skill adds overhead from reading SKILL.md before translating

For Japanese and Spanish, without-skill baseline also performed correctly on all tested assertions — the test file may be too easy for common markup patterns. Consider adding edge cases: nested callout blocks, code chunks with multiple fig.alt options, or prose that contains code-like strings.
